# Ladera Health Watch

> A volunteer, resident-led group in Ladera Ranch, California collecting and
> sharing public records, research, and community updates on self-reported
> cancer cases (including Ewing sarcoma) and pesticide/herbicide use in
> common areas. The group has requested a formal analysis from the
> California Cancer Registry and is working with LARMAC (the community's
> maintenance corporation) on Integrated Pest Management practices.

## Important framing

- No cancer cluster has been officially determined in Ladera Ranch. The
  group does not know what caused any reported cancers, and does not have
  confirmation that local cancer rates exceed the state baseline.
- There is no known environmental cause of Ewing sarcoma.
- The group does not accuse any person, product, company, or agency of
  causing cancer. It describes its work as "asking reasonable questions and
  requesting reasonable changes."
- Ladera Health Watch is a volunteer community group, not a public health
  agency, medical provider, or law firm. Nothing on this site is medical or
  legal advice.

## Key pages

- [What We Know](https://ladera-health-watch.onrender.com/what-we-know/): the full ledger of what is and is not known, a timeline of the effort, and a guide to reading LARMAC's pesticide notices.
- [News & Updates](https://ladera-health-watch.onrender.com/news/): chronological updates from the group and links to press coverage.
- [Report a Case](https://ladera-health-watch.onrender.com/survey/): the official form for reporting a cancer diagnosis for the community registry.
- [Get Involved](https://ladera-health-watch.onrender.com/get-involved/): how to volunteer, and contact information.
- [About](https://ladera-health-watch.onrender.com/about/): who the group is and what the effort is.
- [Community Map](https://ladera-health-watch.onrender.com/map/): a coming-soon map of aggregated, anonymized case data.
